title: R (on the application of A) (FC) (Appellant) v London Borough of Croydon (Respondents) and one other action

metadata.summary: R (on the application of A) (FC) (Appellant) v London Borough of Croydon (Respondents) and one other action R (on the application of M) (FC) (Appellant) v London Borough of Lambeth (Respondents) and one other action
extracts: R (on the application of A) (FC) (Appellant) v London Borough of Croydon (Respondents) and one other action R (on the application of M) (FC) (Appellant) v London Borough of Lambeth (Respondents) and one other action

1. So much depends upon how one frames the question. Put simply, when disputes arise about the age of some-one who is asking a local children’s services authority to provide him with accommodation under section 20(1) of the Children Act 1989, who decides whether he is a child or not? Section 20(1) reads as follows:
